anchors.main_trigger: Chronic and emotional stress, ongoing since Jan 2022

timeline_html:
Summary. Alexsandra has been dealing with hair shedding that began over a year ago and has gradually worsened. Her history shows overlapping emotional and ongoing chronic stress, alongside a major family health event earlier this year.

Your hair-loss triggers.

  • Chronic stress (ongoing; started Oct 2012)
  • Major emotional stress (ongoing; started Jan 2022)
  • Pregnancy, birth, or miscarriage (resolved; May 2015–Feb 2016)
  • Other – Mother’s cancer diagnosis (ongoing; started Mar 2026)

Timeline forecast. The emotional stress that began in early 2022 likely drove the visible hair loss appearing around spring–summer 2022, with shedding peaking near 2022‑04. Based on typical recovery patterns, stabilization would be expected between late 2022 and early 2023, followed by early regrowth around spring 2023. Because chronic stress and ongoing family stress remain active, these recovery windows may stretch slightly. Dominant driver: Chronic and emotional stress overlap since 2022, and the ongoing nature of both keeps the shedding active longer than short‑term triggers.

Where you are now. You’re in the stabilization phase—baby hairs have started appearing, showing early recovery. As stress levels ease and your body regains equilibrium, shedding should continue to calm and density gradually improve. 🌱
